result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single region with the highest cumulative number of affected cities, showing the region's name and the total count.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T3.NumPolicies FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Customer_ID, COUNT(*) AS NumPolicies FROM Customers_Policies GROUP BY Customer_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Customer_ID = T3.Customer_ID WHERE T3.NumPolicies > (SELECT AVG(PolicyCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Policy_ID) AS PolicyCount FROM Customers_Policies GROUP BY Customer_ID) AS T4);

sql: SELECT Country, GenreName, TracksSold FROM (SELECT T5.BillingCountry AS Country, T2.Name AS GenreName, COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS TracksSold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T5.BillingCountry ORDER BY COUNT(T1.TrackId) DESC) as rn FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Invoice AS T5 ON T3.InvoiceId = T5.InvoiceId GROUP BY T5.BillingCountry, T2.Name) AS T6 WHERE T6.rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows identifying the most popular music genre within each country, based on the total number of tracks sold.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, CASE WHEN T2.student_id IS NOT NULL AND T3.candidate_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Both' WHEN T2.student_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Student' ELSE 'Candidate' END AS role FROM People AS T1 LEFT JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id LEFT JOIN Candidates AS T3 ON T1.person_id = T3.candidate_id WHERE T2.student_id IS NOT NULL OR T3.candidate_id IS NOT NULL;

sql: SELECT T4.FirstName, T4.LastName, COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS RockTracksPurchased FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Invoice AS T5 ON T3.InvoiceId = T5.InvoiceId JOIN Customer AS T4 ON T5.CustomerId = T4.CustomerId WHERE T2.Name = 'Rock' GROUP BY T4.CustomerId ORDER BY RockTracksPurchased DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Prominence FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Prominence > (SELECT AVG(Prominence) FROM mountain);
